title: Connect Integrations
lede: Link iMash to the outside apps you already use. The Automation builder connects to external services, and its System Tables actions let a flow read and write your iMash CRM data.

steps.title: Open Automation
steps.content:
1. Go to Intelligence Center → Automation.
  2. Create or open a flow - integrations are added as steps inside a flow.

images.caption: Integrations live inside your automation flows.
Integrations live inside your automation flows.

steps.title: Add an App Step
steps.content:
1. Click the + to add a step and search for the app you want to connect.
  2. Pick the action that app should perform (for example send a message or create a record).

steps.title: Connect Your Account
steps.content:
1. The first time you use an app, you'll be asked to connect it - usually by signing in or pasting an API key.
  2. iMash saves that connection so you can reuse it in future flows.
  3. Choose the saved connection whenever you add that app again.

steps.title: Map Fields and Test
steps.content:
1. Fill in the step's fields, pulling values from earlier steps where needed.
  2. Use System Tables actions to move data in and out of your iMash CRM (leads, contacts, accounts, calls).
  3. Test the step, then Publish the flow so the integration runs automatically.

nextSteps.title: What's Next?
nextSteps.description: Build the flow around your integration:

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io

  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaDescription: Link iMash to external apps inside the Automation builder: add an app step, connect your account, map fields, and use System Tables to sync CRM data.
metaKeywords: integrations, automation, connect apps, system tables, crm sync, credentials, imash
